meta.title: Security | Pulse Appointments
meta.description: How Pulse Appointments protects your business data and your clients' information. Encryption, access controls, PIPEDA compliance, and responsible disclosure.

hero.title: Security
hero.intro: Protecting your business data and your clients' information is foundational to everything Pulse does. Here is how we keep your data safe.

encryption.title: Encryption in transit and at rest
encryption.body: All data transmitted to and from Pulse is encrypted using TLS 1.3. Data stored in our database is encrypted at rest using AES-256. Backups are also encrypted.

passwords.title: Password security
passwords.body: Passwords are hashed with bcrypt (cost factor 12) before storage. Pulse never stores plaintext passwords. Password reset tokens are single-use and expire in 15 minutes.

twofa.title: Two-factor authentication
twofa.body: All user accounts can enable two-factor authentication (2FA) via email or SMS. Business owners are encouraged to enable 2FA on initial setup. Recovery codes are provided at enrollment.

audit.title: Audit logging
audit.body: Security-relevant actions — logins, password changes, staff changes, permission updates, and data exports — are written to a tamper-evident audit log accessible to business owners.

loginAlerts.title: Login alerts
loginAlerts.body: When a sign-in occurs from a new device or IP address, we send an immediate security alert email. The alert includes a one-click password reset link so you can lock your account if it wasn't you.

infra.title: Infrastructure
infra.body: Pulse runs on Railway, a SOC 2 Type II certified cloud platform. Our database is hosted on a managed PostgreSQL service with automated backups, point-in-time recovery, and network isolation.

pci.title: PCI compliance via Stripe
pci.body: Pulse does not store credit card numbers, CVVs, or full payment details. All card processing is handled by Stripe, a PCI DSS Level 1 certified payment processor. Pulse receives only tokenized references.

pipeda.title: Canadian privacy (PIPEDA)
pipeda.body: Pulse is designed for Canadian businesses and built around PIPEDA Schedule 1 obligations — including purpose limitation, data minimization, safeguards, and breach notification. See our Canadian Privacy page for details.

breach.title: Breach notification
breach.body: In the event of a confirmed data breach affecting your business, Pulse will notify affected businesses and clients in accordance with PIPEDA's mandatory breach reporting requirements (within 72 hours of determination).

disclosure.title: Responsible disclosure
disclosure.body: Security researchers who discover a vulnerability in Pulse are encouraged to report it responsibly. We commit to acknowledging reports within 2 business days and to not pursuing legal action against good-faith disclosures.

contact.title: Questions about our security practices?
contact.body: We are happy to answer questions from business owners, enterprise buyers, or privacy officers.

BASIC.name: Basic

PRO.name: Pro
PRO.desc: Automated reminders, no-show protection, and team tools.

UNLIMITED.name: Unlimited

table.footnote: * 1/mo — one automatic fee per calendar month on Free and Basic plans. Upgrade to Pro for unlimited no-show and late-cancel fees.

faq.a: The Free plan has no time limit. Use it forever, then upgrade when you're ready.
Yes. Downgrade or cancel from your account settings at any time. No cancellation fees.

faq.q: What payment methods do you accept?

together.title: Built to work together
together.body: The strongest Pulse workflows combine multiple features: an intake form before booking, a deposit at checkout, SMS reminders before the appointment, and a review request after completion.

meta.title: Online Booking Software for Canadian Service Businesses | Pulse
meta.description: Let clients book appointments online 24/7. Pulse gives you a free public booking page — add it to Instagram, Google, or your website. No app download required. CAD pricing.

og.title: Online Booking Software | Pulse Appointments
og.description: Free online booking page for Canadian service businesses. Works on Instagram, Google, and any website.

online-booking.title: Let clients book appointments online,
online-booking.description: No more phone tag. No more DMs asking "are you free Thursday?" Pulse gives you a free public booking page your clients can use any time — from Instagram, Google, or your website.

steps.title: Add your services
steps.desc: Enter your service names, prices, duration, and availability. Takes about 5 minutes. No technical skills required.

steps.title: Share your booking link
steps.desc: Put your Pulse booking link in your Instagram bio, Google Business profile, website, or email signature. Clients tap the link and book themselves.

steps.title: You get notified, they get confirmed
steps.desc: Pulse sends you a notification and the client a confirmation email instantly. The appointment appears in your calendar. Done.

features.title: Works anywhere clients find you
features.body: Your booking page works from your Instagram bio, your Google Business listing, a link in your email, or embedded on your website. One link, everywhere.

features.title: Mobile-first for clients
features.body: Clients book from their phone in under 2 minutes, no app download required. They pick a service, choose a time, and pay a deposit if required — all in one flow.

features.title: Multi-staff and multi-service
features.body: Clients can choose their preferred provider and service. Each staff member gets their own calendar and availability. No double-bookings possible.

faqs.q: Do clients need to create an account to book?
faqs.a: No. Clients book using just their name, email, and phone number. No password, no account, no app download required. The simpler the booking flow, the more bookings you get — that's why we designed it this way.

faqs.q: Can I embed the booking page on my existing website?
faqs.a: Yes. Pulse provides an embeddable booking widget you can add to any website with a single line of code. Clients book directly on your site without being redirected to a separate page. Works with Squarespace, Wix, WordPress, and any custom site.

faqs.q: What happens if a client tries to book outside my working hours?
faqs.a: Pulse only shows your actual available slots. If you're not working on Sundays, Sunday slots don't appear. If a slot is already booked, it's removed from the calendar in real time. Clients can only book times that genuinely work for you.

online-booking.ctaText: Your booking page is live in under 5 minutes. Free to start — no credit card required.

meta.title: Booking Deposit Software for Canadian Service Businesses | Pulse
meta.description: Require a deposit when clients book online. Pulse automatically collects and holds deposits — and charges no-show fees to card-on-file. CAD pricing, free to start.

og.title: Booking Deposit Software | Pulse Appointments
og.description: Collect deposits at booking and charge no-show fees automatically. Built for Canadian service businesses.

deposits.title: Require a deposit when clients book —
deposits.description: Stop losing money to no-shows. Pulse collects a deposit when clients book and charges the card automatically if they don't show up. Set it up once. It runs itself.
proofPoints: No credit card required to start

steps.title: Set your deposit amount
steps.desc: Choose a flat dollar amount or a percentage of the service price. Set it per-service — a colour treatment can require more than a quick trim.

steps.title: Client pays at booking
steps.desc: When a client books online, Pulse collects the deposit through Stripe. The client sees the deposit amount and policy before confirming.

steps.title: No-show? Charge automatically
steps.desc: If a client no-shows or cancels inside your policy window, Pulse charges the remaining balance (or the full no-show fee) to the card automatically. No awkward calls.

deposits.featuresTitle: Everything you need to protect your revenue

features.title: Per-service deposit rules
features.body: Set different deposit amounts for different services. A 3-hour colour appointment can require a $50 deposit. A 20-minute trim can have none.

features.title: Card-on-file for no-show fees
features.body: After the deposit is collected, the client's card stays on file. Late cancel or no-show? The additional fee is charged automatically — you never have to ask.

features.title: Revenue Protected dashboard
features.body: See exactly how much money Pulse has saved you from no-shows and cancellations. Your Revenue Protected number shows the real ROI of deposits.

faqs.q: What happens if a client disputes the deposit charge?
faqs.a: Stripe's built-in dispute process handles chargebacks. Pulse provides a record of the deposit policy the client agreed to at booking — this is the most important evidence in a dispute. Chargeback rates for deposits with clear upfront policies are very low.

faqs.q: Can I refund a deposit if a client cancels with enough notice?
faqs.a: Yes. You can refund deposits manually from your Pulse dashboard at any time. If a client cancels within your free-cancellation window, you can refund the deposit in one click. Pulse doesn't refund automatically — you stay in control.

faqs.q: Do clients need to create an account to pay a deposit?
faqs.a: No. Clients pay the deposit as part of the booking flow using any credit or debit card, Apple Pay, or Google Pay. No account creation required. The card is stored securely by Stripe for future charges.

deposits.ctaText: Set up deposits in under 5 minutes. Free to start — no credit card required.

meta.title: No-Show Protection for Canadian Service Businesses | Pulse
meta.description: Automatically charge no-show and late cancellation fees to card-on-file. Pulse enforces your cancellation policy so you never have to have an awkward conversation again.

og.title: No-Show Protection Software | Pulse Appointments
og.description: Enforce cancellation policies and charge no-show fees automatically. Built for Canadian service businesses.

no-show-protection.title: Charge no-show fees automatically —
no-show-protection.description: Pulse enforces your cancellation policy for you. When a client no-shows or cancels last minute, the fee is charged to their card on file automatically. You get a notification. They get a receipt. No conversation needed.
proofPoints: Works for salons, spas, barbers, and more

steps.title: Set your cancellation policy
steps.desc: Choose your cancellation window (e.g. 24 hours) and fee amount (flat fee or percentage). Pulse shows the policy to every client before they confirm a booking.

steps.title: Client agrees at booking
steps.desc: By completing their booking, the client agrees to your cancellation policy. Their card is stored securely by Stripe. No surprises later.

steps.title: Fee charged automatically
steps.desc: If a client no-shows or cancels inside your policy window, Pulse charges the fee automatically and sends both of you a receipt. You don't have to do anything.

no-show-protection.featuresTitle: Protection that works while you work

features.title: Policy enforcement without confrontation
features.body: You set the rules once. Pulse enforces them automatically. No chasing clients, no uncomfortable calls, no letting people off the hook because you felt awkward asking.

features.title: Customizable cancellation window
features.body: Set your policy to trigger at 24 hours, 48 hours, or any window that suits your business. Late-cancel fees and no-show fees can be set independently.

features.title: Revenue Protected — see your real savings
features.body: Your Revenue Protected dashboard shows exactly how much Pulse has recovered for you. Most businesses recover their entire monthly subscription cost in the first week.

comparison.feature: Client agrees to policy at booking

faqs.q: What if a client claims they didn't know about the cancellation policy?
faqs.a: Every client sees and agrees to your cancellation policy when they book — it's displayed on the booking page and included in their confirmation email. This written agreement is your protection. Clients who claim ignorance are contradicted by the booking record.

faqs.q: Can I waive a no-show fee for a specific client?
faqs.a: Yes. You can cancel or refund any automatically charged fee from your dashboard. The system charges automatically, but you always have the final say. Some businesses waive the first offence and charge from the second.

faqs.q: Does no-show protection work for in-person and online bookings?
faqs.a: It works for all bookings made through Pulse — whether clients book through your public booking page, your Instagram bio link, or a booking link you send them directly. Walk-in bookings entered manually by you can also have card-on-file added at the time of the appointment.

no-show-protection.ctaTitle: Stop absorbing the cost of no-shows
no-show-protection.ctaText: Set up no-show protection in under 5 minutes. Free to start — no credit card required.

meta.title: SMS Appointment Reminders for Canadian Service Businesses | Pulse
meta.description: Automated SMS and email appointment reminders that reduce no-shows by up to 80%. Pulse sends reminders at 72h, 24h, and 2h before every appointment. CAD pricing.

og.title: SMS Appointment Reminders | Pulse Appointments
og.description: Automated SMS and email reminders that cut no-shows. CASL-compliant, built for Canadian service businesses.

sms-reminders.title: Automated SMS reminders
sms-reminders.description: Email reminders get ignored. SMS reminders get opened. Pulse sends automated text reminders before every appointment — reducing no-shows by up to 80% without any manual work from you.

steps.title: Turn on SMS reminders
steps.desc: Enable SMS reminders in your Pulse settings. Choose which intervals to use: 72 hours, 24 hours, and 2 hours before each appointment. Takes 60 seconds to set up.

steps.title: Clients opt in at booking
steps.desc: When a client books, they provide their phone number and consent to receive appointment reminders. CASL-compliant consent is captured automatically.

steps.title: Reminders go out automatically
steps.desc: Pulse sends SMS messages at exactly the intervals you set. No manual work. Each message includes the appointment time, service, and a link to reschedule or cancel.

sms-reminders.featuresTitle: Reminders that work while you work

features.title: SMS + email, both channels
features.body: Some clients prefer text. Some prefer email. Pulse sends both so every client gets reminded in the way they actually check. The 24h SMS is the one that stops no-shows.

features.title: Customizable reminder timing
features.body: Use the default 72h/24h/2h schedule or adjust to what works for your business. A busy salon might want a 48h reminder. A mobile groomer might add a same-morning text.

features.title: CASL-compliant consent capture
features.body: Canada's anti-spam law (CASL) requires consent before sending marketing or transactional messages. Pulse captures this automatically at booking — you're covered.

faqs.q: What plan do I need for SMS reminders?
faqs.a: SMS reminders are available on the Basic plan ($19 CAD/month) and above. The Free plan includes email confirmations and updates, but not scheduled SMS or email reminders. You can upgrade at any time from your account settings.

faqs.q: Does Pulse handle CASL compliance for SMS automatically?
faqs.a: Yes. When a client provides their phone number at booking, Pulse captures their consent to receive appointment reminders in a CASL-compliant format. You don't need to manage consent separately. Marketing messages (campaigns, win-backs) have separate consent captured under your marketing settings.

faqs.q: What does the reminder SMS actually say?
faqs.a: Pulse sends a plain-language message with the client's name, appointment date and time, service name, and your business name. It includes a link to their booking details where they can reschedule or cancel. You can customize the message template from your notification settings.

sms-reminders.ctaTitle: Cut no-shows by up to 80% — automatically
sms-reminders.ctaText: Enable SMS reminders in under 60 seconds. Free to start — no credit card required.

features.title: Appointment history

features.title: Client messages

features.title: Fast lookup

faqs.q: What client details can I store in Pulse?
faqs.a: Pulse stores client contact details, appointment history, notes, intake answers, messages, invoices, reviews, and booking preferences where available.

faqs.q: Can staff see client notes?
faqs.a: Owners and authorized staff can access client details from the dashboard so the team has context before each appointment.

faqs.q: Is client management included on the free plan?
faqs.a: Yes. The free plan includes client management basics. Paid plans add more operational tools such as reminders, deposits, no-show protection, and advanced team features.

client-management.ctaTitle: Bring client context into every appointment
client-management.ctaText: Start free and keep your booking records, client details, and history connected from day one.

intake-forms.title: Collect client details before
intake-forms.description: Ask the right questions at booking: allergies, preferences, goals, service notes, or consent details. Pulse keeps intake answers with the appointment so your team is prepared.

steps.title: Client answers while booking
steps.desc: The intake step appears inside the booking flow, so details are collected before the visit.

steps.title: Staff review before service
steps.desc: Answers stay connected to the appointment and client record for faster, more prepared service.

features.title: Custom intake questions
features.body: Collect the exact details your business needs instead of forcing a generic form.

features.title: Appointment context
features.body: Answers stay tied to the booking, making pre-appointment review simple for staff.

features.title: Trust documentation
features.body: Pulse supports public privacy and security pages so clients understand how the booking experience is handled.

faqs.q: Can I create custom intake questions?
faqs.a: Yes. Pulse supports custom intake questions so businesses can collect the details that matter for their services.

faqs.q: When do clients complete intake forms?
faqs.a: Clients complete intake questions during the booking flow, before the appointment is confirmed.

faqs.q: Are intake answers connected to the appointment?
faqs.a: Yes. Intake answers are connected to the appointment and client context so staff can review them before the visit.

intake-forms.ctaTitle: Collect better details before every appointment
intake-forms.ctaText: Start free and add intake questions to your booking flow without a separate form tool.

meta.title: Multi-Location Booking Software for Canadian Service Businesses | Pulse
meta.description: Manage multiple locations, staff calendars, service availability, and online booking links in Pulse. Built for Canadian service businesses.

og.title: Multi-Location Booking Software | Pulse Appointments
og.description: Manage service booking across multiple business locations and staff calendars.

multi-location.title: Run booking across multiple
multi-location.description: Whether you have two studios, rotating pop-ups, or multiple service areas, Pulse helps keep locations, staff, calendars, and client booking paths organized.

steps.title: Add your locations
steps.desc: Create each studio, service area, or operating address in your business settings.

steps.title: Assign services and staff
steps.desc: Keep staff schedules and service availability aligned with where the work actually happens.

steps.title: Clients book the right place
steps.desc: Clients see the right location context while your team keeps calendars organized behind the scenes.

features.title: Location management
features.body: Keep multiple studios, rooms, pop-ups, or service areas organized under one Pulse account.

features.title: Calendar clarity
features.body: Reduce scheduling mistakes by separating where appointments happen from who provides them.

features.title: Local expansion ready
features.body: Built for Canadian businesses growing from solo operator to team and multi-location operations.

faqs.q: How many locations can I manage in Pulse?
faqs.a: Pulse supports multiple locations on paid plans. Pro supports 2 locations and Unlimited supports up to 5.

faqs.q: Can each location have different staff and availability?
faqs.a: Yes. Locations can be managed with their own operational context, staff schedules, and booking setup.

faqs.q: Is multi-location useful for mobile service providers?
faqs.a: Yes. Location support helps businesses separate service areas, studios, pop-ups, or different operating addresses.

multi-location.ctaText: Start free, then upgrade when your business needs multi-location booking controls.

meta.title: Review Management Software for Canadian Service Businesses | Pulse
meta.description: Collect and publish real appointment reviews from completed visits. Review management for Canadian service businesses using Pulse.

og.title: Review Management Software | Pulse Appointments
og.description: Collect, moderate, and publish reviews tied to real appointments.

reviews.title: Collect real reviews after
reviews.description: Pulse helps you earn trust the right way: send review requests after real appointments, moderate feedback, and publish reviews where new clients are deciding whether to book.

steps.title: Appointment is completed
steps.desc: Review requests are connected to actual appointment activity, not invented marketing copy.

steps.title: Client leaves feedback
steps.desc: Clients can rate the experience and add a written comment from a signed review link.

steps.title: You publish what is useful
steps.desc: Owners can publish or hide reviews from the dashboard before they appear publicly.

features.title: Review request workflow
features.body: Turn completed appointments into timely review requests without manual follow-up.

features.title: Moderation controls
features.body: Review feedback privately and choose what appears publicly on your booking page.

features.title: Public trust signal
features.body: Published reviews help new clients make a booking decision without relying on placeholder testimonials.

faqs.q: Does Pulse create reviews automatically?
faqs.a: No. Pulse sends review requests after completed appointments, but clients choose whether to leave feedback.

faqs.q: Can I choose which reviews are public?
faqs.a: Yes. Owners can publish or hide reviews from the dashboard before they appear publicly.

faqs.q: Where do published reviews appear?
faqs.a: Published reviews can appear on the public booking page so prospective clients can evaluate the business before booking.

reviews.ctaText: Use completed appointments to build public trust without publishing fabricated testimonials.
